watercolors.title: Landscape
watercolors.description:
Akvarelmaleri af landskab, udført af giver A.L.'s fars moster, malerinden M.R. (f. 1872). Maleriet findes også som olie- på lærred hos giver. 
Genstanden er udstillet i Kvindemuseets permanente udstilling "Kvindeliv fra urtid til nutid" i montre 1 vedrørende "Uddannelse og borgerret" frem til oktober 2014.

Maleriet forestiller en landevej med kornmark til højre, træer, buske og el-master til venstre. Marker i det fjerne. På bagsiden står med blyant: "Hvorpå der sidder fugle". Udført af Minna Ruge. 

Intakt. Overfladen i venstre side er skallet af. På bagsiden mange brune pletter.

watercolors.title: Landworkers, Landscape
watercolors.description:
Watercolor, with motifs on both sides.

On the first page are seen 4 rural workers working on the field. The two front workers are a man and a woman. The man is seen in gray work pants, beig-colored shoes, brown jacket and green cap. The woman is seen with a red scarf around her head, gray dress, beig-colored stockings and white clogs. They both have a working tool in their hands.
On the other hand, a landscape consisting of fields, trees and houses is seen.
There is a lack of signature.

watercolors.title: Landworkers, Landscape

On the first page are seen 4 rural workers working on the field. The two front workers are a man and a woman. The man is seen in gray work pants, beig-colored shoes, brown jacket and green cap. watercolors.title: Landscape, 1942
watercolors.description: Akvarel af Gunver Bilving. Landskabsbillede malet i 1942. Det fremstår i brunlige farver.

watercolors.title: Landscape with Cows
watercolors.description:
Engstykke on Hasselø, facing the strait. On the piece is seen a man/farmer from behind sitting on a stone. The man has his face turned to 4 grazing cows. The horse that is glimpsed out to the right is close to the picket that closes off the meadow.
At the bottom of the right corner is the following handwritten text “Otto Larsen. 1926.”
At the back of the watercolor is pencil drawing, which probably depicts one of Rodin’s sculptures. The figure depicting a man in 2/3 figure is seen with his chin pressed against his chest. He is naked and wearing only headgear. 
At the bottom of the left corner is a somewhat indistinct handwritten text “Rodin f.... 100?”
At the bottom, two stamps are seen. The first stamp will be seen with the text “KUNSTAKADEMIET SKOLERAAD.” The second stamp is seen with the text “14.DEC.1926.”

watercolors.title: Raben, Fjord Landscape, 1922
watercolors.description: Watercolor of a fjord landscape with sun-drenched mountain peaks in the background. Signed J. Raben. 1922 in n.v.h. Narrow black drawn frame.

watercolors.title: Watercolor, Landscape with Trees
watercolors.description:
Glasindrammel watercolor imagined a landscape with trees, fields and a farm with a red roof.
The watercolor is set up on a piece of carton and this is joined by the glass with an adhesive strip all the way around the picture.
The glass is cracked, so the upper vein. corner had gone off - is put together with tape.
The watercolor is signed in ve. bottom corner: “Sheep Sealant 1942 - Anne Lise Reuter Lorentzen.” and hay. bottom corner: “To Gudrun Felsing.”

watercolors.department: Metropolitan Organisation of Museums of Visual Arts of Thessaloniki – MOMus
watercolors.credit_line: Metropolitan Organisation of Museums of Visual Arts of Thessaloniki – MOMus
watercolors.description: Την περίοδο αυτή ακμάζουν οι πρωτοπόροι καλλιτέχνες της "Εταιρείας των Περιπλανώμενων" και ο νεότερος κλάδος τους, οι καλλιτέχνες που προσανατολίζονται προς το Συμβολισμό, όπως ο Βρούμπελ και ο Μπορίσοφ-Μουσάτοφ, οι εμπρεσιονιστές της Μόσχας από την ομάδα της "Ένωσης Ρώσων Καλλιτεχνών", η ένωση "Ο Κόσμος της Τέχνης", οι συμβολιστές καλλιτέχνες της δεύτερης γενιάς από την ένωση "Γαλάζιο Ρόδο", η οποία αποτέλεσε την αφετηρία των νέων καλλιτεχνικών τάσεων. Το 1910 εμφανίζονται οι καλλιτέχνες της ομάδας "Βαλές Καρό" στη Μόσχα και σχηματίζεται η "Ένωση Νεολαίας" στην Πετρούπολη, που συγκεντρώνει στις εκθέσεις της όλους τους καλλιτέχνες της ρωσικής πρωτοπορίας.

smooth_oils.title: Landscape Oil painting on canvas.
smooth_oils.description:
In the foreground on the left tree group, on the right temple ruin, some people. In the middle background skin, bridge. In the background mountainous landscape.
Kilad span frame. On the span frame “G.Brahe,” with white chalk 3.5 cm h. Gilded frame width 85 mm. 
Signature on the back of the canvas: “Myller Fecit Holmiae 1710” with yellow color. 7 cm h. and, at the bottom, obscure text with yellow colour.

smooth_oils.title: Landscape. Oil painting on canvas.
smooth_oils.description:
In the foreground trees, temple ruin and dormant adorable people. In the middle ground fortified city. In the background blue mountains.
Cloudy sky. Painted in a sketchy mane. 
The canvas overweight about 150 mmm around the tension frame eftr right edge. On the left edge of the canvas Swedish newsprint, possibly from 1839. 
Kilad span frame. On the tension frame patch with text “Chiepolo or Ricci.” Gilded frame convex profile and metal corner fittings, width 85 mm.

smooth_oils.title: Landscape. Oil painting on canvas.
smooth_oils.description:
In the foreground trees, people and a caravan with loaded donkeys, in the middle ground blue mountains. Molning sky. Painted in a slightly sketchy mane. The cloth overweight about 80 mm around the tension frame eftr right edge, eftr left edge about 100 mm. The paper of the Swedish newspaper, possibly from 1839 after the right edge. 
Kilad buckle frame. On the buckle frame patch with text “Chiepolo or Ricci.” Gilded frame convex profile and metal corner fittings, width 85 mm.

smooth_oils.title: Oil painting
smooth_oils.description: Painting executed in oil depicting a soldier named Helge who is in an idyllic winter landscape brought in uniform m/1910 and armed with a rifle. The painting lacks a frame.

smooth_oils.title: on canvas. Genre painting.
smooth_oils.description:
Till vänster havsvik med skepp, till vänster därom Beerenberg på Jan Mayens land. Till höger på strand grönlandsval och ett trankokeri, ett stort antal personer. Danska flaggor på skeppen och på stranden. 
Signatur: "A. R Spoeck 1634", "C 4" vitt mot rött på husgavel.

Ny kilad spännram. Svart, profilerad ram, bredd 117 mm.

Danish Whaling Station.
The painting depicts a view over the Danish whaling cookery on the island of Danskoya at Spitsbegen, Svalbard. The cookery was run by the two brothers Johannes and Gothard Braem, both of them depicted at the down center of the painting. The motif shows the full process of the whale-oil industry during the first part of the 17th century. At the far left the actual hunt is being conducted from ships bearing the Danish flag and the flag of the order  Den væpnede arms, while the center and right focus on the different steps of producing the whale-oil, with the two brothers making a deal with a merchant in the center of the foreground.

The struggle for control over the whaling industry was of huge economical and political importance to both Denmark and the Netherlands. This is illustrated by the reproduction of Danish Whale Station in 1639 by Cornelis de Man entitled Whale-Oil Factory of the Amsterdam Chamber of the Northern Company at Smerenburg, now in the Rijksmuseum in Amsterdam. In this painting by de Man the motif and composition are the same, but all signs of the factory being Danish are here replaced with Dutch flags and ships.
The painting was most likely taken as war booty by Carl Gustaf Wrangel in 1658 during the war between Sweden and Denmark, possibly from Nyborg Castle.

The painting is signed and dated A.B.R SPEECK 1634. Although there are no records of an artist active with that name at that time it is likely that the name corresponds to an Abraham Speeck of Amsterdam who was a builder and an inventor of fireworks who also supposedly worked as a painter. Records shows that he was involved with the whaling industry at Spitsbergen as an inventor of rocket propelled harpoons tested there in 1638. The connection between the name, the geographic location, the period of time and the occupation makes it highly plausible that this Abraham Speeck is the artist.

smooth_oils.title: on canvas
smooth_oils.description:
Constantia Koskull, 1788-1840. May be Mariana E. Koskull, 1765-1823.
Waist image, phase right.
Short curly hair, yellow sleeveless chemise dress. Over the left shoulder red shal.
Hot Carnation. In the background landscape.
Kilad span frame.
Gilded profiled frame with pastel agelists. 
The width of the frame list 105 mm.
Behind the canvas “C Cool” coated, 1 cm h, with ink, and bluntly “480” with chalk, 10 cm h.
Behind the frame patch: “The Friherrdess Constantia Koskull Gift 1. with Mr. Johan Hall in Gothenburg, divorced 1811.2. 1822 with Friherre Wedel in Denmark.”

smooth_oils.title: Allegory. Oil painting on canvas.
smooth_oils.description:
Two men in Roman costumes take each other in hand, one holds a balance wave with waves and others in the waves. Landscape in the foreground brown, in the background bruised. 
Inscription: “AMICITIAE TRVTINA.” 
The canvas nailed on black simple profiled frame, width 60 mm.

smooth_oils.description:

Inscription: “NÄSWIJS.”
Stifted and wedged span frame.
Brown Marbled Frame, Width 120 mm.
Three carved streaks on the back of the frame.

smooth_oils.title: on a boiler. Dutch Winter Landscape with Sledding and Skating People.
smooth_oils.description:
Holländskt landskap. Vinterbild med släd- och skridskoåkande människor. Oljemålning på pannå. Mörkbrun profilerad ram, listens bredd 108 mm. Signerad med ihopskrivet JS på kälke till vänster.

Inköpt av ministerpresidenten i Haag, Harald Appelblom, på tavelauktion i Haag 3 juli 1651 för Carl Gustaf Wrangels räkning.

Winter scene is one of the earliest known paintings by Steen. With its diagonal composition and silhouetted figures on the ice one can clearly see his early inspirations from paintings such as Isaac van Ostades Winter from 1645. Here, as often seen in other works by Steen and his contemporarys, the activites are being watched by a well dressed couple who occupies a central position in the composition.
The painting was bought on behalf of Carl Gustaf Wrangel by the Swedish ambassador Harald Appelbom at an auction in The Hauge on 3 July 1651. During that same auction Appelbom also bought three other paintings by Jan Steen for Wrangel described as "A rich mans kitchen", "A poor mans kitchen" and "Hagar". The first and the second most likely corresponds with the paintings "The fat Kitchen" (now in private collection) and "The lean Kitchen" (now in the National Gallery of Canada, Ottawa). The third one however is more difficult to trace, it is possible that it is the same painting as the one now entitled "Hagar in the desert".

smooth_oils.title: Women. Called “The Borderline of Sabines.”  Oil painting on canvas.
smooth_oils.description:
Three men, three women and two horses. Strong movement nedif4robbery left up towards right. In the background architecture and landscape. 
Dark colors. 
new wedged span frame (1969). 
Gilded profiled frame with pastel law ornaments, width 65 mm.

smooth_oils.title: Halsnöy Monastery near Bergen, Norway. Oil painting on canvas.
smooth_oils.description:
I fonden åtta vitmålade hus och ruinerna av en större byggnad. Bruntonat landskap med sjö, brygga och två timrade fiskarbodar. En båt på sjön och på stranden en metande man. I förgrunden två bönder med spadar och två kvinnor. Under denna grupp inskrift :  " HALSNÖE  KLOSTER  A :  OSTEN  : B :  W.  OTTE : KRAEG : HANS : BUGNING  C : KIRSKEN : TO : HAVEN : LANG : D : BORREST  VEN : E : POORTEN : F : SKOGLEN : G. FAEHUSET : H.  LADEN  I :  NÖSTED : K : SÖBOD : L. BRYGEN  -- 1656 E.F. ".
Signerad :  " E(lias)  F(igenschou) ".
Otydlig , texten enligt Hans Nerhus.
Kilad spännram.
Svartmålad fururam. Ramlistens bredd 90 mm. Wrangellapp :  " No 590 denisch Kloster . . . . ".

smooth_oils.title: Karl XII, painted by Julius Kronberg in 1893.
smooth_oils.description:
Man portrait, full picture, phase, body slightly v, head slightly h, in the blue Fredrikssstens uniform, v. hand at the waist, h. hand holds it in the ground supported sword, h foot supports against the stump. Behind the stump a lion with one paw on a three-crown castle. In the background landscape with city to h. Above the king one in heaven hovering Fama, full figure, phase, head facing backwards to v and blowing in the rumor trumpet with a trumpet fan with Karl XII’s name cipher. In h. She holds a laurel wreath. Fluttering costume, barefoot, helmet with plymage. Signing down to t. h. Julius Kronberg 1893. Gilded ornamental frame with loose crown at the top, royal crown held by two hovering putti (mounted on plate dressed with blue velvet).

Belonging to the Nordic Museum.

smooth_oils.title: Poppy, Paul: Campagna, landscape, oil on canvas, 60 x 109 cm, Rome 1867
smooth_oils.description: Poppy, Paul: Campagna, landscape, oil on canvas, 60 x 109 cm, Rome 1867

smooth_oils.title: Madame La Comtesse de Saint Geran. Oil painting on canvas.
smooth_oils.description:
Madame La Comtesse de Saint Geran. (Francoise Madelaine Claude de Warignes 1667 g.m. Bernhard de la Guiche, Count of Saint-Géran).
Full figure to horse. Red brocade dress, black, hip long jacket, gold and silver embroidered, lined with green fabric as well as the dress. Piece scarf and cuffs with blue rosettes. Red hat with a fur board and jewelry. In the hands are sprinkled and rein. Horse with red, gold embroidered saddle cover. In the fund the castle Versailles, the stalls in the foreground.

On the canvas: “Me La Comtesse De S.t Geran.”
On the back of the canvas: “N57 Pour Monseigr Le Duc Daumont.”
In the frame patch: “Princesse Chantillon.”
Gilded, carved frame with acanthus leaves. A silvered mussel on either side. The width of the frame list 115 mm. Top of the back of the frame “53” with pencil.
New wedged span frame. Double cloth.

Equestrian portrait of Francoise Madeleine Claude St.Géran, born de Warignes, with a view of the Palace of Versaille in the Equ.
The belongs to a series of six at Skokloster Castle, all the ladies of the French high court on horseback with belviews in the ongs for Louis Marie Victoire, Duc d 'Aumont. The paintings given as a married to or by the Swedish embassy Count Nils Bielke during his paintin Paris 1679-82. The series later lav transtransfrom Bielkes castle at Salsta to Skokloster Castle in 1756. All six paintings bear the option Pour Monseigr Le Duc Daumont on the back of the canvas.
